[[lat.md/competition-invites#Competition Invites#Candidates query]] (lat.md/competition-invites.md:71-76)

> ## Candidates query
>
> [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/candidates.ts#getCandidatesForSourceComp]] is the source-of-truth read for the championship roster's per-`(sourceComp, sourceDivision)` fan-out. It returns active registrations as `CandidateEntry[]` with athlete name + email already hydrated, ordered by `registeredAt`.
>
> The query joins `competition_registrations` → `users` (inner) → `scaling_levels` (left), filtered by `eventId`, `divisionId`, and `status ≠ "removed"`. There is no track-workout, heat, event-status, or publication gating — every active registration in the division surfaces. This is the architectural fix for the missing-divisions bug: invite candidacy is "is this athlete registered in the division" not "did this athlete score on a published event in a heat." Score-based ranking inside the candidate set is a follow-up; today rows are stable-ordered by `registeredAt`.
>

input.description: Search for invite status display logic

content.content:
[[lat.md/competition-invites#Competition Invites#Registration hand-off + Stripe workflow]] (lat.md/competition-invites.md:217-230)

> ## Registration hand-off + Stripe workflow
>
> [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts]]'s `initiateRegistrationPaymentFn` accepts an optional `inviteToken`. When supplied:
>
> - The token is resolved + claimability-checked + email-matched against the session — any of these failing short-circuits with an error that the registration form can surface.
> - The submitted `items` must be exactly the invited division — `items.length === 1 && items[0].divisionId === invite.championshipDivisionId`. Invites are pinned per division, so the server rejects both wrong-division submissions and "invited division plus extras". The registration form locks the UI to match (single read-only division, no multi-select) when `inviteToken` is present.
> - The registration-window check is skipped for invite-holders (invites often precede public-open or survive past close). The form mirrors this — the `!registrationOpen` disable is bypassed for both the submit button and the form inputs (division select, affiliate, team fields, waiver toggles, question fields) when the invite lock is active. Otherwise the submit unlocks but the required fields stay frozen, and the invitee can't actually fill anything in.
> - The single item's purchase metadata gets `inviteId` tagged so the Stripe workflow can flip the invite on payment success.
> - The per-(source, division) allocation guardrail runs before the Stripe session is created — same `assertInviteWithinAllocation` + `getOccupiedCountForBucket` pair the claim landing uses. This closes the `/register?invite=…` direct-submit hole where an athlete with a valid invite for an already-full bucket would otherwise pay-and-refund. Bespoke invites (sourceId IS NULL) bypass.
>
> [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/workflows/stripe-checkout-workflow.ts]] reads `inviteId` from the purchase metadata inside `create-registration`, threads it through `RegistrationStepResult`, and runs a new `update-competition-invite-status` step after registration creation. The step flips the invite to `accepted_paid`, sets `paidAt`, sets `claimedRegistrationId`, and nulls `claimToken` so a replay of the original email link short-circuits. Idempotent via the `status = "pending"` guard on the update predicate so a workflow retry doesn't double-flip. `processCheckoutInline` (local-dev path) runs the same helper to keep behavior consistent without Cloudflare Workflows.
>
> When a 100%-off coupon zeroes the total, the registration server fn takes the `allFree` branch and skips Stripe entirely — so it mirrors the workflow flip inline right after creating the registration row. Without this, an invite-locked free checkout would leave the invite stuck in `pending` even though the athlete is registered.
>

## This section references:

* [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts]] (apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts)
* [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/workflows/stripe-checkout-workflow.ts]] (apps/wodsmith-start/src/workflows/stripe-checkout-workflow.ts)

[[lat.md/competition-invites#Competition Invites#Invites schema]] (lat.md/competition-invites.md:103-112)

> ## Invites schema
>
> Each invite is a per-athlete row in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/competition-invites.ts#competitionInvitesTable]] carrying the email-locked claim token, status, and origin attribution. ULID primary keys via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/common.ts#createCompetitionInviteId]].
>
> The `activeMarker` column is the correctness pivot: it holds the literal `"active"` while `status IN (pending, accepted_paid)` and is NULL on every terminal state. Combined with the unique index `(championshipCompetitionId, email, championshipDivisionId, activeMarker)` this enforces "at most one active invite per (championship, division, email)" while letting declined/expired/revoked rows accumulate (MySQL treats multiple NULLs as distinct). The `claimToken` column is plaintext `varchar(255)` with its own unique index (`competition_invites_claim_token_idx`) — same shape as [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/teams.ts#teamInvitationTable]]'s `token` column, looked up directly via `eq()` rather than hashed. Terminal transitions null the token so historical rows coexist under the same multi-NULL semantics. `sendAttempt` (default 0) is the re-send counter that drives the Resend `Idempotency-Key` suffix; reusing `invite.id` across re-issues stays safe because the suffix rotates.
>
> Draft bespoke rows (created before any send) use a distinctive shape: `activeMarker = "active"`, `claimToken = NULL`, `expiresAt = NULL`, `emailDeliveryStatus = "skipped"`. The unique-active index blocks duplicate bespoke adds for the same (championship, division, email). Secondary indexes cover the organizer table queries — `(roundId)`, `(sourceId)`, `(origin)`, `(status)`, `(email)`, plus `(championshipCompetitionId)` and `(userId)` for roster-side joins.
>
> Phase 2 uses `roundId = ""` as a sentinel pending the Phase 3 `competition_invite_rounds` table; the column is NOT NULL with a default of `""` so a Phase-3 backfill can rewrite every Phase-2 row to a synthetic "Round 1 — Backfill" without schema gymnastics.
>

[[lat.md/competition-invites#Competition Invites#Sent invites tab]] (lat.md/competition-invites.md:85-96)

> ## Sent invites tab
>
> The Sent tab is an audit view that groups every issued invite (active OR terminal) by championship division. Reads the `AuditInviteSummary` projection from `listAllInvitesFn`; no mutations live here.
>
> The component is [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/sent-invites-by-division.tsx#SentInvitesByDivision]] and the server fn is [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-invite-fns.ts#listAllInvitesFn]]. `listAllInvitesFn` is intentionally distinct from `listActiveInvitesFn` rather than a widening of it: the Candidates tab's bespoke-draft list relies on the active filter to keep terminal history out of the staged-drafts list — widening the existing fn would push that filter onto every caller, which is exactly the foot-gun the active-marker pattern was designed to avoid.
>
> The new fn returns an [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-invite-fns.ts#AuditInviteSummary]] DTO that extends `ActiveInviteSummary` with `divisionLabel` (joined from `scalingLevels`), `sourcePlacementLabel` (denormalized "1st — Comp · Div" for source-origin rows), and `lastUpdatedAt` (mapped from the row's `updatedAt` — the table has no dedicated `lastSentAt` column today and `updatedAt` is the closest available signal of "last activity" because it bumps on every issue / reissue / dispatch / status flip).
>
> The component renders one card per division using the loader's `championshipDivisions` ordering. Each card carries a status counter row computed from the *unfiltered* per-division set — counters tell you what's in the bucket; the page-level filters narrow the table inside. Page-level filters are status chips, origin chips, and a free-text name/email search. Per-division empty-state copy renders when filters narrow rows out, so the organizer always sees the full division layout. The single per-row action is "copy live claim link" for rows where `claimUrl !== null` — revoke / reissue / resend stay in the round builder (Phase 3 / sub-arc D).
>
> ADR-0012 Phase 2: the per-division headline denominator (`divisions[].maxSpots` on the component prop) reads `divisionAllocationTotals[divisionId]` — the resolved sum of per-source invite allocations for that championship division — sourced from [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-invite-fns.ts#listInviteSourceAllocationsFn]] in the route loader. It is no longer the championship's `competition_divisions.maxSpots` (registration capacity). The per-source breakdown chips read their `allocated` value from a loader-supplied `allocationsBySourceByDivision: Record<sourceId, Record<championshipDivisionId, number>>` map; the component no longer parses `competitionInviteSourcesTable.divisionMappings` JSON at render time. A division with a resolved total of `0` falls back to the "X accepted" rendering (no denominator), matching the prior optional-field signal.
>

Per-division empty-state copy renders when filters narrow rows out, so the organizer always sees the full division layout. The single per-row action is "copy live claim link" for rows where `claimUrl !== null` — revoke / reissue / resend stay in the round builder (Phase 3 / sub-arc D).
>
> ADR-0012 Phase 2: the per-division headline denominator (`divisions[].maxSpots` on the component prop) reads `divisionAllocationTotals[divisionId]` — the resolved sum of per-source invite allocations for that championship division — sourced from [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-invite-fns.ts#listInviteSourceAllocationsFn]] in the route loader. It is no longer the championship's `competition_divisions.maxSpots` (registration capacity). The per-source breakdown chips read their `allocated` value from a loader-supplied `allocationsBySourceByDivision: Record<sourceId, Record<championshipDivisionId, number>>` map; the component no longer parses `competitionInviteSourcesTable.divisionMappings` JSON at render time. A division with a resolved total of `0` falls back to the "X accepted" rendering (no denominator), matching the prior optional-field signal.
>

Found it. The DB is correct — `zac+invite1@wodsmith.com`'s invite is `status="accepted_paid"`, `paid_at=2026-05-01 05:47:39`, with a `claimed_registration_id`. The Stripe→workflow flip worked.

The bug is in the UI: `StatusPill` (`championship-roster-table.tsx:298`) only has two states — "Invited" / "Not invited". `isRowAlreadyInvited` returns true for **any** active invite (including `accepted_paid`), so paid registrants still render as "Invited". We need a third state for `accepted_paid`.

Let me check the props plumbing then fix it.

content.content:
123:Threat model: the token already lives forever in the recipient's email inbox and the email provider's outbound logs, so a DB hash was never the only or even primary copy of the secret. The actual auth gate is `identityMatch` — even with a stolen token, the visitor must be signed in as the invited email address (verification-gated sign-up if no account exists) to claim. The token is an unguessable identifier scoped to a recipient, not a bearer password. Defense-in-depth: terminal transitions (`accepted_paid`, `declined`, `revoked`, `expired`) null `claimToken` so a leaked active row cannot replay after payment, and the organizer can copy the live claim URL straight from the invite list UI to resend or hand-deliver — the user-visible feature that motivated the change.
153:[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-invite-fns.ts#getInviteByTokenFn]] also cross-checks `competition_registrations` when the visitor is signed in as the invited identity: a non-removed row for `(eventId, userId, divisionId)` short-circuits to `not_claimable` with reason `already_paid`. This is the correct defense because it works regardless of which lane (public, organizer-manual, or prior invite claim) created the registration — a still-`pending` invite for an already-registered athlete should not re-enter the payment flow. **This cross-check only fires when the token still resolves**: it sits inside `getInviteByTokenFn` after `resolveInviteByToken`, so it covers stale-but-still-`pending` invites, not paid-then-replayed links.
183:The invite variant renders [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/registration/invite-division-hero.tsx#InviteDivisionHero]] in place of the picker, pins the invited division as the only selection, and bypasses the public registration window — the invitation itself is the authorization to register. If the URL points at a division that's no longer eligible (full, already-registered, removed) the variant transparently falls back to the public flow so the athlete can still pick something else rather than seeing nothing.
222:- The submitted `items` must be exactly the invited division — `items.length === 1 && items[0].divisionId === invite.championshipDivisionId`. Invites are pinned per division, so the server rejects both wrong-division submissions and "invited division plus extras". The registration form locks the UI to match (single read-only division, no multi-select) when `inviteToken` is present.
235:Three dialogs sit in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/add-bespoke-invitee-dialog.tsx]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/bulk-add-invitees-dialog.tsx]], and [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/send-invites-dialog.tsx]]. Add/Bulk dialogs wrap the bespoke staging server fns; Send wraps `issueInvitesFn`. The route header carries the three buttons (Add invitee / Bulk add / Send invites N); Send is disabled when no recipients are selected. The Send dialog computes its defaults locally from the championship + division props passed by the parent route — subject defaults to `You're invited to {championshipName} - {divisionLabel}` and the body textarea defaults to a generic invitation message — so organizers don't have to retype either on every send. The greeting line (`Hey {athleteName},`) is rendered automatically per-recipient by [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/react-email/competition-invites/invite-email.tsx#CompetitionInviteEmail]] above the body, so the textarea holds the message-only portion.
241:[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/organizer/invites/championship-roster-table.tsx]] now accepts optional `selectedKeys` / `onToggleSelection` / `onToggleAll` props. When the parent supplies them a checkbox column appears; rows with no `athleteEmail` are disabled with a "No email on file" tooltip so the organizer can't try to invite an athlete without a resolvable address. The table also takes an optional `isRowAlreadyInvited(row)` callback — when it returns true the row's status pill renders "Invited" instead of "Not invited", the checkbox is disabled with an "Already invited" tooltip, and the row is excluded from select-all so the "Send invites (N)" chip can't silently disagree with the visible checked-box count. [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/roster.ts#RosterRow]] now carries `athleteEmail` — hydrated at the end of `getChampionshipRoster` via a single bulk `userTable` lookup so the Send dialog has an email to put on each recipient.
253:The register route's `validateSearch` accepts both `invite` (the plaintext token) and `divisionId` (the invited division — invites are locked to one division at issue time). The route forwards both into `RegistrationForm` as the `inviteToken` and `inviteDivisionId` props. When both are present and the division resolves against the competition's scaling group, the form treats the registration as invite-locked: it pre-seeds `selectedDivisionIds = [inviteDivisionId]`, seeds the team-entry map for team divisions, and replaces the multi-select dropdown with a read-only "Invited Division" display. The closed-window banner and submit-button "Registration Closed" copy are also bypassed in this mode so the registration-window-skip from the server fn isn't visually contradicted. Combined with the purchase-metadata + Stripe-workflow wiring from sub-arc C, this closes the Organizer Send → athlete claim → Stripe → `accepted_paid` loop end-to-end.
259:The register route loader, on receiving `?invite=<token>`, calls a local `getInvitePrefillFn` server function that resolves the token (re-using [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/claim.ts#resolveInviteByToken]]), authorizes the caller by requiring the session user's normalized email to match the invite's normalized email (the prefill payload exposes other people's emails and names, so the token alone is not enough — `getInviteByTokenFn`'s public lookup only returns the invited identity's own metadata), verifies the URL slug against the invite's championship to guard against pasted-in-wrong-tab errors, and delegates to [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-invites/prior-team.ts#getPriorTeamForInvite]]. That helper uses `invite.sourceCompetitionId` to find the invitee's `competition_registrations` row in the prior comp; when `athleteTeamId` is set, it reads the prior `teamName` and joins `team_memberships` → `users` to project `{email, firstName, lastName, affiliateName}` for every other active member. The captain (= invitee) is filtered out via `ne(userId, inviteeUserId)`. Bespoke invites (`sourceCompetitionId IS NULL`) and individual prior registrations (`athleteTeamId IS NULL`) return `null` and the form falls back to empty teammate slots. When `invite.userId` isn't denormalized (older invites issued pre sign-up), the helper resolves the user by normalized email; if no account exists yet, it returns `null` rather than guessing.

239	The bulk dialog only exposes an "emails — one per line or comma-separated" affordance even though the underlying `parseBespokePaste` helper still understands the richer CSV/TSV row shape. Before sending, it splits the textarea on `[\n,]+` and rejoins with newlines so a single comma-separated line becomes one email per row (otherwise the helper's CSV parser would treat trailing emails as name fields). The primary CTA reads "Invite", closes the dialog on success, and surfaces the created/duplicate/invalid counts via a sonner toast — `toast.warning` when there are duplicates or invalid lines, `toast.success` otherwise.

Before sending, it splits the textarea on `[\n,]+` and rejoins with newlines so a single comma-separated line becomes one email per row (otherwise the helper's CSV parser would treat trailing emails as name fields). The primary CTA reads "Invite", closes the dialog on success, and surfaces the created/duplicate/invalid counts via a sonner toast — `toast.warning` when there are duplicates or invalid lines, `toast.success` otherwise.

The same payload still carries a `hasClaimToken` boolean so the draft-vs-already-sent distinction is a single field check rather than an emptiness test.

content.text:
All checks pass. 

**Summary**

- **DB is correct**: `cinv_01KQH0RFPEP5DH60VMMRG10WBK` for `zac+invite1@wodsmith.com` is `status=accepted_paid`, `paid_at=2026-05-01 05:47:39`, with `REDACTED`. The Stripe→workflow flip worked.
- **Bug is in the UI pill**: `championship-roster-table.tsx`'s `StatusPill` was binary — "Invited" / "Not invited" — and `isRowAlreadyInvited` returned `true` for **any** row with `activeMarker = "active"`. Since `accepted_paid` keeps `activeMarker = "active"`, paid registrants kept showing as "Invited".
- **Fix**: replaced `isRowAlreadyInvited(row) → boolean` with `getInviteStatusForRow(row) → "pending" | "accepted_paid" | null`. Pill now renders **"Registered"** (sky tint) for `accepted_paid`, **"Invited"** (emerald) for `pending`, **"Not invited"** otherwise. Selection logic unchanged — any non-null status still disables the checkbox. Updated `lat.md/competition-invites.md` to match. Type-check + tests green, `lat check` clean.
